name: Flounder in Lemon-Dill Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. In a 10-inch skillet over medium-low heat, melt the butter or margarine and stir in the dill, dill weed, lemon juice, and salt until combined.
2. If the flounder fillets are large, cut them into serving-sized pieces. Add the flounder to the skillet and cover. Cook for 5-8 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the flounder flakes easily when tested with a fork, basting it frequently with the butter mixture.
3. Arrange the cooked flounder on a warm platter and garnish with lemon slices.
